Prep: rinse the chicken under cold water
crack the eggs into a bowl
gather the ingredients on the counter
dry the fish with a paper towel
remove the seeds from the pepper
drain the water from the beans
Cut: chop the onion into small pieces
slice the tomato into thin pieces
trim the fat from the meat
grate the cheese into a bowl
divide the dough into small balls
Mix: mix the ingredients in a bowl
whisk the eggs in a bowl
combine the flour and water in a bowl
blend the ingredients in the food processor
mix the salad in a large bowl
Cook-Heat: heat some oil in a pan
cook the chicken in a pan
fry the chicken in hot oil
simmer the sauce in the pot
grill the patties on the grill
melt the butter in a pan
add the vegetables to the wok and cook
flip the pancake and cook the other side
Bake: bake the dish in the oven
roast the vegetables in the oven
put the tray in the oven
place the baking sheet in the oven
cook the pizza in the oven
bake the chicken until cooked through
roast the meat on a baking tray
cook the chicken wings in the oven
Season: season the dish with salt and pepper
add salt and pepper to season the dish
sprinkle black pepper on the chicken
rub the meat with salt and spices

rub spice powder onto the chicken
Plate: serve the dish on a plate
place the cooked food on a serving plate
transfer the cooked pasta to a serving bowl
arrange the vegetables on the plate

put the cooked chicken on a plate
place the finished dish on a serving tray
garnish the plated food with herbs
Idle: let the dough rest for ten minutes
let the mixture sit in the bowl
wait until the sauce cools down
repeat the same steps for the remaining pieces
let the meat marinate in the refrigerator
continue the process with the rest

notes: This draft narrows Season around salt, pepper, spices, seasoning, sprinkle, and rub-spice contexts.
Ingredient-only expressions such as garlic, basil, soy sauce, and curry powder were avoided as Season prototypes.
Prep removes broad add, place, pour, and coat-style prototypes that pulled container-transfer sentences into Prep.
Cook-Heat strengthens pan, pot, wok, oil, boil, fry, simmer, grill, and cook contexts.
Plate emphasizes serve, plate, garnish, serving plate, serving bowl, and finished-dish presentation.
Idle remains narrow and focuses on waiting, resting, cooling, repeating, and setting aside.
